Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We have a long question from user which describes an exercise regarding loan application process It is divided into two parts Part 1 Normative Process Behavior with strict rules about sequence of events Part 2 Desiderata or recommended practices that are less important and not necessarily violations

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Check strict rules for case1 Sequence is Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Approved Inform Applicant Finalize Documentation Close Case Rules Credit check before risk assessment Risk assessment before loan decision Inform applicant after loan decision If approved then finalize documentation before closing So case1 satisfies strict rules

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Check sequence Strict rules for case2 Must do Credit Check before Risk Assessment but here risk assessment happened at 0820 and credit check occurred later at 0900 So thats a violation of normative order Also Finalize Documentation should not be done if loan decision is rejected yet there is finalization documentation performed after the rejection decision Thus violation of normative rules for case2 in two aspects

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Check normative rules for case3 It is an approved loan decision For an approved loan we require that Finalize Documentation occurs before closing Yes it does Also check ordering of events Application Received Credit Check Risk Assessment Loan Decision Inform Applicant Finalize Documentation Close Case So all normative rules are followed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Thus for part 1 Violating normative rules Case2 violates Risk Assessment performed before Credit Check and Finalize Documentation executed after loan decision rejected Case1 and case3 satisfy strict sequence order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Desiderata are recommended best practices such as performing Credit Check within 24 hours of Application Received inform applicant promptly within 48 hours from the loan decision and follow up call before closing

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case1 Check timing differences Application received at 0900 Jan 10 credit check is at 0930 which is well within 24 hours Inform applicant at 1300 Jan 10 which is less than 3 hours after loan decision so thats fine The only desideratum issue might be missing Followup Call

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case2 The credit check is performed within 24 hours of application received But case2 never has an Inform Applicant event and there is no follow up call So normative problems dominate and missing follow up call can also be treated as a best practice deviation

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case3 Credit Check occurred more than 24 hours after Application Received so that is a desideratum violation Inform Applicant happened later than recommended 48 hours from loan decision so that is another desideratum violation Also case3 does not have a follow up call event

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Summary Case1 Normative compliant Desiderata missing Followup Call possible Case2 Normative violations risk assessment before credit check missing Inform Applicant and finalizing documentation on rejected case Desiderata missing Followup Call possible Case3 Normative compliant Desiderata delayed Credit Check delayed Inform Applicant and no Followup Call

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: For case2 It appears there was a mistake in ordering the credit check and risk assessment It might be due to oversight or system error and finalizing documentation despite rejection suggests that process automation failed to branch properly

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: For case3 The delays in performing Credit Check and Inform Applicant could indicate resource constraints or procedural backlog however it is not strictly non compliant but suboptimal performance relative to recommended timelines
